meaning: adj.主妇似的,主管的,安祥的,尊严的
examples: A matronly head appeared around a door.
Her matronly fame was trodden under all men's feet.
Above the bed in his tiny, cigarette-strewn hotel room hangs a framed photo of him and the matronly AIDS worker.
Mr Blair presented his policy in a speech at St Thomas' hospital, beneath a pair of portraits of matronly women.
Wilcox is a restaurant owner with an earnest, matronly air.
